data.title: Arbutus falls as patent dispute with Moderna hits EU snag
data.description:
Arbutus Biopharma (ABUS) lost ~14% in the premarket on Friday after the company disclosed that the Board of Appeal of the European Patent Office revoked one of its patents, which was the subject of a legal dispute between the company and Moderna (MRNA).

Moderna (MRNA) added ~4% in reaction as Arbutus (ABUS) said that the decision will impact cases brought by the company and its licensing partner, Genevant Sciences, against the COVID-19 vaccine maker in Switzerland and in the Unified Patent Court.

The ruling, communicated verbally, follows the Notices of Opposition submitted by the company in connection with the ’254 Patent originally filed by Moderna (MRNA) and Merck (MRK).

After receiving written communication regarding the ruling over the coming months, Arbutus (ABUS) expects to file a petition for review, the Warminster, Pennsylvania-based biotech said in a regulatory filing.

data.title: Applied Materials, AEI Industries, MKS in focus as KeyBanc ups targets
data.description:
Applied Materials (AMAT), AEI Industries (AEIS), and MKS (MKSI) were in focus on Friday as KeyBanc Capital Markets upped its price targets on the trio, noting that the semiconductor industry is poised for a strong 2026.

“We recently attended the SEMI Industry Strategy Symposium in San Francisco,” KeyBanc analysts wrote in a note to clients. “Our first takeaway is that it is now consensus that semiconductor sales will hit $1T this year, or next at the latest. Last year, the most bullish case was 2028 with consensus anchored on 2029-2030. The shift is understandable – seemingly limitless demand for GPUs driven by inexhaustible [data centers] budgets, the 2nm node transition, and memory pricing, which has doubled. These dynamics seem well reflected in share prices, but when the narrative shifts to Super Cycle, you have to be there. To be sure, we heard some obligatory cautionary tones, but essentially no one thought the music is about to stop.”

For AEI Industries, KeyBanc reiterated its Overweight rating and upped its price target to $280 from $240, as the surge in the share price over the past 9 months looks set to continue. 

“While shares have seen a significant run since April, we think much of the performance has been driven directly by AE’s [data center] exposure, and we continue to see multiple other ways for AEIS to win in the next few years,” the analysts wrote. “In Semi, we have yet to see a material impact from new product introductions, which should solidify its RF positioning in conductor etch and could lead to share gains in dielectric over time. As such, we are increasing our 2027 and 2028 segment revenue estimates to 11% and 10%, respectively. The Industrial & Medical end market has been under pressure for multiple years, and a resurgence would not only add to top line but would benefit mix as well. Further, we remind investors that AEIS remains 'on the hunt' for an I&M acquisition, and we think the high end of its aspirational targets could add up to $500M sales and $1.25 EPS post-synergies. We also see AEIS’s operational strategy adding a continuing, gradual benefit to margins over time, with a [long-term gross margin] target of [more than] 43% from today’s high-30%s.”

The analysts also kept their Overweight rating on Applied Materials and upped their price target to $380 from $285, as the company has lagged its peers, KLA Corp. (LKAC) and  Lam Research (LRCX).

“We think that more muted expansion is a function of AMAT’s higher exposure to trailing edge nodes for both China and [rest of world] customers,” the analysts wrote. “We understand that underperformance but note AMAT is the most diversified supplier and should benefit from increasing deposition and etch intensity as chip architecture at the leading edge (to eventually include DRAM) goes vertical. It is also the name most exposed to conventional DRAM, arguably the AI-related device facing the most scarcity, which we think at some point should translate into capacity expansion. We also expect AMAT’s Applied Global Services segment should post [low-double-digit] revenue growth in FY27-FY28 due to customers running the installed base of equipment at high utilization levels. While we acknowledge the front-end names generally perform as ‘one trade’, we think AMAT should screen better for new money given its relative valuation and integral position in enabling semiconductor manufacturing.”

The analysts also raised their price target on MKS, moving to $250 from $180 and kept their Overweight rating.

“We are not particularly concerned about MKS’s leverage given its FCF profile, and we like MKS’s broad sub-system exposure and think it should benefit from leading edge buildout over the next few years,” the analysts said. “Additionally, MKS power sources are incumbent on most NAND etch tools, meaning it should benefit from NAND upgrade spend even if greenfield capacity is not added in the [near-term]. In Advanced Packaging, we like MKS’s 'drill & fill' portfolio for PCBs and substrates, where technological advances are driving some of the most impactful benefits to power and performance in advanced chips, and also where there is little existing capacity to serve that market. We think these factors will combine to drive accelerating revenue and EPS growth fueled by cyclical recovery in semiconductor sales and secular growth in advanced packaging. Because of these factors, combined with its relatively lower multiple premium compared to the group, MKSI remains our preferred way to play this portion of the semicap upcycle.”

data.title: UK MHRA approves higher weekly dose of Novo Nordisk's Wegovy
data.description:
• The UK's Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency has approved a maximum weekly dose of Novo Nordisk's (NVO) obesity medicine Wegovy (semaglutide) of 7.2mg per week.
  • It is given as three 2.4 mg injections to individuals with a body mass index of 30kg/m² or higher.
  • The starting dose for Wegovy is 0.25mg per week but can be titrated upwards.

data.title: Meritage Homes, Taylor Morrison downgraded to Neutral from Buy at BofA
data.description:
Meritage Homes (MTH) and Taylor Morrison Home (TMHC) were downgraded to Neutral from Buy at BofA Securities on downside risks to 2026 earnings.

"Homebuilder stocks have rallied sharply year-to-date (+13% vs. S&P 500 +1%) after underperforming the market in 2025 in response to lower mortgage rates and investor enthusiasm around potential favorable housing policy actions," said the BofA analysts.

"While investors often focus on mortgage rates, we believe the weaker employment and migration trends, ongoing inflation (particularly lot costs), and a more competitive selling environment driven by elevated new and resale inventory will pressure fundamentals through 2026," said the research note.

"We view 2026 as a 'reset year' where the industry reduces spec inventory and adjusts underwriting assumptions on land—setting the stage for potential recovery in 2027," said the research note.

"We expect ROE to decline across most builders in our coverage universe, and we are reducing our 2026 EPS estimates by 6%, reflecting a more conservative gross margin outlook," said the analysts.

"While sentiment may improve following upcoming housing-policy announcements, we believe lower mortgage rates are unlikely to meaningfully lift entry-level demand. Builders are already aggressively buying down rates, and any decline in mortgage rates is likely to increase resale inventory—creating additional competition for new-home sales," said the research note.

For Meritage, BofA sees downside risk to consensus 2026 gross margin expectations, given its higher exposure to entry-level product and ongoing land inflation. And, on account of the recent share price rally, valuation no longer screens compelling.

Taylor Morrison Home screens attractively on valuation, but the investment bank sees the most downside risk vs. consensus EPS among builders on lower deliveries.

TMHC shares were -1.87% Friday pre-market to $62.95.

BofA's rating on Meritage Homes aligns with Seeking Alpha's Quant rating of Hold. SA authors, as well as sell-side analysts, grade the stock as Buy.

Quant sees Taylor Morrison as Hold, but SA analysts grade the stock as Strong Buy. Wall Street analysts rate TMHC as Buy.

data.title: Regions Financial stock drops after disappointing Q4 results, underwhelming guidance
data.description:
Regions Financial (RF) stock slid 4.2% in Friday premarket trading after the bank's Q4 earnings missed consensus as noninterest income fell short of the average analyst estimate. Furthermore, its 2026 guidance signals that results could fall short of the current consensus estimates.

The bank expects 2026 net interest income growth of 2.5%-4% from 2025's $4.99B, with fixed-rate asset turnover, funding cost management, and loan growth as the primary drivers. The guidance implies $5.11B-$5.19B, compared with the Visible Alpha consensus of $5.19B.

It also expects adjusted noninterest income growth of 3%-5% for the year from 2025's $2.54B, implying $2.62B-$2.67B vs. Visible Alpha's estimate of $2.68B.

For Q1, it sees net interest income declining 1%-2% from Q4's $1.29B, and net interest margin to be mostly stable at ~3.70%. That implies Q1 NII of $1.26B-$1.28B vs. VA consensus of $1.26B. Regions (RF) sees NIM trending higher during the year, reaching low/mid 3.70%s by Q4 2026.

Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.57, missing the average analyst estimate of $0.61, fell from $0.63 in Q3 and $ in Q4 2024.

Q4 adjusted revenue of $1.92B, trailing the $1.94B consensus, declined from $1.94B in the prior quarter and increased from $1.82B a year ago.

Net interest income (fully taxable equivalent) of $1.29B, in line with the Visible Alpha consensus, increased from $1.27B in Q3 and $1.24B in the year-ago Q4. Net interest margin ((FTE)) of 3.70% compared with 3.59% in Q3 and 3.55% in Q4 2024.

Adjusted noninterest income dipped to $640M, missing the VA estimate of $646M, from $684M in the previous quarter and increased from $615M in the year-ago period.

Provision for credit losses rose to $115M from $105M in Q3 and fell from $120M in Q4 2024.

Adjusted noninterest expense of $1.11B was roughly unchanged from Q3 and increased from $1.03B in the year-ago Q4.

Average deposits increased to $129.9B from $129.6B in the prior quarter.  Average loans of $95.7B slipped from $96.7B in the previous quarter.

"While operating in a competitive environment, and in many of the strongest markets in the country, our teams delivered solid growth in 2025 by attracting more clients across our lines of business and generating record-breaking results in Wealth Management and Treasury Management," said Chairman, President, and CEO John Turner. "We see improving underlying trends in the nation's economy, further supporting the momentum we've built and strengthening our foundation for solid performance in 2026."

Conference call at 10:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Regions Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.57 misses by $0.04, revenue of $1.92B misses by $20M

data.title: Groq said to reach out to buyers for cloud unit: report
data.description:
• Artificial intelligence semiconductor startup Groq has begun the process of reaching out to prospective buyers of its cloud unit, Bloomberg reported.
  • The company is working with a financial adviser to sell GroqCloud and could get as much as $1B or more for the unit, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with eh matter. GroqCloud sells subscriptions that provide the opportunity to developers to rent time on the company's AI hardware. 
  • Among the potential buyers include companies that are looking to boost their AI capabilities, such as large language models and public semiconductor companies, the news outlet added, citing its sources.
  • Groq did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Nvidia (NVDA) announced in late December that it entered into a non-exclusive licensing agreement with Groq, worth some $20B, for its inference technology. As part of the agreement, Groq’s Founder Jonathan Ross, President Sunny Madra and other members of the Groq team, joined Nvidia.

data.title: American Axle & Manufacturing lands approval in China for GNK Automotive acquisition
data.description:
China's State Administration of Market Regulation approved American Axle & Manufacturing's (AXL) purchase of GKN Automotive owner Dowlais Group

The roughly $1.4B cash-and-stock transaction aims to create a larger, more diversified global driveline and metal-forming supplier with greater exposure to electrified powertrains. The deal is framed as a strategic combination in which American Axle & Manufacturing's (AXL) shareholders are expected to own about 51% of the combined company and Dowlais (GKN Automotive’s parent) shareholders about 49%, with a partnership structure that preserves Dowlais investors’ participation in potential upside from integration and industry consolidation.

Looking ahead, the combined company aims to benefit from greater scale as the auto industry grapples with volatile demand for electric vehicles and the global expansion of Chinese electric vehicle makers. American Axle & Manufacturing's (AXL) management has guided to approximately $300 million of synergies or profit improvement within about three years of closing, with a focus on scale benefits in drivelines, broader OEM diversification, and cross-selling across internal combustion, hybrid, and EV platforms.

The acquisition is expected to be completed in the first quarter, subject to remaining regulatory and shareholder approvals.
